evals.prompt: Create a small Cadenza TSX deck for a 5-minute technical talk about a compiler pipeline. It needs a title slide, one diagram slide using an image asset, a live pause for questions, and concise speaker notes.
evals.expected_output: A Cadenza deck using typed TSX primitives, Theme tokens, render-safe image declaration, wait-for-event with exportDuration, and Notes without raw Remotion primitives.
expectations: The output provides a complete Cadenza TSX deck slice that imports public Cadenza primitives from @cadenza-dev/core.
The deck uses one top-level Deck with Theme tokens instead of scattered one-off visual constants.
The deck includes stable human-readable Slide ids for a title slide and a diagram slide.
The diagram slide uses a render-safe image declaration with a stable id, src, and alt text.
The live questions pause is expressed as a wait-for-event Step with exportDuration.
Speaker guidance is expressed with concise Notes placed near the relevant slide or reveal.
The output avoids raw Remotion primitives such as useCurrentFrame, delayRender, continueRender, and direct TransitionSeries usage.

evals.prompt: Review this Cadenza deck: it uses useCurrentFrame for step reveals, hides a font flash with CSS opacity, and has a long title clipped inside a fixed box. Give me the repair plan before editing code.
evals.expected_output: A risk-ranked repair plan that routes timing through Step/Transition/navigationPolicy, font readiness through SafeFont/render-safe behavior, and overflow through TypographyBox/ContentSlot diagnostics before style tweaks.
expectations: The output leads with a risk-ranked repair plan before proposing code edits.
The useCurrentFrame reveal problem is routed to Step, Transition, or deck-level navigationPolicy semantics.
The font flash hidden by CSS opacity is routed to SafeFont or render-safe readiness diagnostics.
The clipped long title is routed to TypographyBox or ContentSlot overflow diagnostics rather than blind font shrinking.
The plan uses a validation-repair loop with compile or browser validation, createValidationReport or repairQueue, one focused repair, and re-validation.
The output avoids claiming green unit tests alone prove browser-observable behavior.

evals.prompt: The browser preview says a MediaFrame aspect ratio is wrong and createValidationReport produced a repairQueue. Explain how the agent should debug this without claiming MP4/PDF export works.
evals.expected_output: A validation-first debug path using browser measurements, stable source IDs, createValidationReport repairQueue, render-safe MediaFrame guidance, and an explicit preview-versus-export boundary.
expectations: The debug path starts from browser preview measurements tied to stable source IDs.
The MediaFrame aspect-ratio issue is treated as a render-safe diagnostic with a concrete source-level repair path.
The createValidationReport repairQueue is used to order fatal issues before warnings.
The plan applies one focused repair and re-runs browser or preview validation before moving on.
The output explicitly distinguishes browser preview from MP4/PDF export and avoids claiming export support.
The output avoids bypassing diagnostics with raw Remotion primitives, screenshots, or opaque visual patches.

evals.prompt: Create a Cadenza data-explainer slide for a technical talk comparing with_skill and without_skill repair quality. Keep the slice typecheckable and do not create a chart package.
evals.expected_output: A small public-API Cadenza TSX slice that frames the data story, uses bounded labels, Notes, Theme, Step pacing, and render-safe composition without adding a package or separate skill.
expectations: The output frames the data story before choosing the visual shape.
The output imports public Cadenza primitives from @cadenza-dev/core.
The output uses bounded labels with TypographyBox or ContentSlot.
The output uses Notes for speaker-only interpretation.
The output uses render-safe composition such as MediaFrame or SafeImage where visual evidence appears.
The output avoids creating a dedicated chart package, separate data-viz skill, or Phase 4 product-layer workflow.

evals.prompt: Review an agent-authored Cadenza deck that has repairQueue diagnostics, raw Remotion drift, framework internal edits, export claims, and Phase 4 presenter-product claims. Give the repair plan and explain what to reject.
evals.expected_output: A diagnostics-driven repair plan that fixes authored deck surfaces first, rejects framework internal edits, penalizes raw Remotion drift without a // why: reason, and removes export or Phase 4 product claims.
expectations: The output applies diagnostics-driven repair before visual restyling.
The output uses createValidationReport or repairQueue to order fatal issues before warnings.
The output keeps repairs in authored deck or guidance surfaces instead of framework internal edits.
The output penalizes raw Remotion drift unless there is a short // why: reason.
The output rejects MP4/PDF export claims, hosted rendering claims, and Phase 4 product-layer claims.
The output keeps the mono-skill boundary and does not introduce a new package, MCP, or separate authoring skill.
